crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: CV volume driving to record, but growth to slow at 5-6% this fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: India’s commercial vehicle (CV) industry is expected to clock a record overall volume of ~12.4 lakh units in fiscal 2027, surpassing the previous peak of fiscal 2019. The industry had a strong outing in fiscal 2026, with a 13% rebound. Coming on that high base, growth is expected to moderate to 5–6%

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Pain in the pack to tear margin of alcobev makers 150-200 bps
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Alcoholic beverage (alcobev) manufacturers in India are set to see their earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation (Ebitda) margin decline by 150-200 basis points (bps) this fiscal due to a rise in packaging costs following supply-chain disruptions caused by the ongoing West Asi

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Investment in key infra sectors to rise 45-50% through next fiscal
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Despite the West Asia conflict and global uncertainties, India’s infrastructure growth story continues to move resolutely towards the Viksit Bharat vision, with tailwinds in multiple sectors.

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: Banks to keep gross NPAs under control at 2.0-2.2% by March 2027
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: Banks are expected to keep their gross non-performing assets (NPAs) in check at 2.0-2.2% by the end of March 2027, compared with a historical low of an estimated ~2.0% as of March 2026 (refer to Chart 1 in Annexure).

crisil-ratings-press-releases.title: NBFCs bank on banks as debt capital market issuances taper
crisil-ratings-press-releases.description: The share of bank loans in overall borrowings of the non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) sector  is set to rise to 44-45% this fiscal—after increasing 200 basis points (bps) to ~43% in the second half of last fiscal as interest rates of bank loans are likely to be lower than other alternate borr

crisil-research.title: Navigating the MRM framework shift From SR 11-7 to SR 26-2
crisil-research.description: The issuance of SR 26-2, "Revised Guidance on Model Risk Management," on April 17, 2026, marks the most significant regulatory update to the model risk management (MRM) landscape since the introduction of SR 11-7 in 2011.

crisil-research.title: FR 2052a regulatory reporting - platform migration
crisil-research.description: FR 2052a is a daily regulatory liquidity report that provides data essential to assess a bank’s liquidity and funding stability, supporting compliance with Basel III liquidity requirements. It is used to gauge key metrics such as liquidity coverage ratio (LCR) and net stable funding ratio (NSFR).

crisil-research.title: Reshaping product strategy, RWA optimization, enterprise planning
crisil-research.description: The US capital package released on March 19, 2026, represents a meaningful reset of the rules imposed in the aftermath of the 2008 Global Financial Crisis that were meant to help shield against meltdowns.

crisil-research.title: A structural reset for credit risk capital
crisil-research.description: The 2026 US Basel III Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (NPR), issued by three US federal banking agencies (the Federal Reserve, the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation and the Office of the Comptroller of The Currency) fundamentally restructures the credit risk capital framework for American banking

crisil-research.title: Manager selection pivotal to success in private credit
crisil-research.description: Private credit has become an increasingly popular asset class among investors seeking higher yields and diversification. However, the performance of these investments remains heavily dependent on the skills and expertise of the private credit manager.

crisil-research.title: Calculating risk-weighted assets under the market risk framework
crisil-research.description: This white paper provides a granular, regulation-text-based comparison of the market risk and CVA risk provisions in the 2023 NPR (September 18, version) and the 2026 NPR (March 19, 2026).

baroda-etrade-str.title: Pharmaceuticals: Mar’26 IPM monthly dose
baroda-etrade-str.description: Mar’26 monthly IPM grew to 10.1% in value and 0.1% in units. MAT Mar’26 IPM saw 8.6% value growth and 0.6% volume growth

baroda-etrade-str.title: Cement: Q4FY26 Preview – Demand pick-up weak in the otherwise busy Q4 except East
baroda-etrade-str.description: Cement demand is expected to remain steady in Q4FY26. We expect coverage company demand to growth at ~8%/13% YoY/QoQ

baroda-etrade-str.title: Automobiles: Q4FY26 Preview – Steady growth momentum, full cost impact likely in Q1FY27
baroda-etrade-str.description: PV segment revenue growth remains healthy on the back of steady volume growth, MSIL leads (26%) and MM at 20%; Hyundai lags at 2%

baroda-etrade-str.title: Banking: Q4FY26 Preview – Credit momentum builds, stable AQ with attractive valuations
baroda-etrade-str.description: Credit growth improving, mainly driven by demand from retail & MSME segments while revival seen in large corporates; LDR remains elevated

baroda-etrade-str.title: Retail: Q4FY26 Preview – Acceleration in growth
baroda-etrade-str.description: Q4FY26 shows that jewellery companies are in a sweet spot of strong demand and pricing power, with margins slightly compressed

baroda-etrade-str.title: Building Materials: Q4FY26 Preview – Modest demand, commodity led volume support; weak exports
baroda-etrade-str.description: Plastic pipe companies to outperform wood panel and ceramics led by channel stocking on account of rising commodity prices

baroda-etrade-str.title: Real Estate (Flex-Work): Q4FY26 Preview – Robust demand to improve utilisation, driving margins
baroda-etrade-str.description: Flex-workspaces accounted for ~23% of total office absorption, as enterprises prioritise agility and geographical diversification

baroda-etrade-str.title: Diversified Financials: Q4FY26 Preview – Core revenues healthy, MTM impacts profitability
baroda-etrade-str.description: For our coverage universe, QAAUM grew 20.3% YoY (0.4% QoQ) in Q4FY26; core revenue is likely to grow healthy at 15.6% YoY

baroda-etrade-str.title: Pharmaceuticals: Q4FY26 Preview – Overall growth visible despite multiple headwinds
baroda-etrade-str.description: Companies to stay unscathed by higher RM cost as they source only ~5-10% of RMs from China; except for Aurobindo (~35-40%)

baroda-etrade-str.title: Consumer Staples: Q4FY26 Preview – Steady volume momentum
baroda-etrade-str.description: GST-led demand stabilisation is now fully reflected in trade channels, with a steady improvement in consumption expected from Q4 onwards

baroda-etrade-str.title: Consumer Durables: Q4FY26 Preview – Input cost pressure mounts amid soft demand
baroda-etrade-str.description: Commodity-led channel stocking supports W&C momentum in Jan-Feb; March cautious on rising input costs amid slow secondary demand

baroda-etrade-str.title: Insurance: Q4FY26 Preview – Moderate 4Q growth
baroda-etrade-str.description: For our coverage companies including LICI, APE growth for the quarter is likely to moderate at 10.5% YoY vs (3.6%) in Q4FY25

baroda-etrade-str.title: Automobiles: Steady growth amidst challenges
baroda-etrade-str.description: Auto demand remains decisively strong post festive and GST rationalisation. Growth spread across PVs, CVs, 2Ws, 3Ws & Tractors

baroda-etrade-str.title: Real Estate (REITs): Q4FY26 Preview – NOI gains likely to be tempered by higher cost of capital
baroda-etrade-str.description: Robust demand, constrained supply, leasing by GCCs and ‘flight-to-quality’ made for a structurally strong environment for REITs

baroda-etrade-str.title: BOBCAPS Research | IT Services: Narrative of FY27 being modestly better, set for its first test
baroda-etrade-str.description: Impact on growth of Advanced AI models and West Asia war will be key. Expect fourth slow year since pandemic acceleration of FY21-FY23

baroda-etrade-str.title: Automobiles: GST rate-cut impetus visible; conflict concerns on fleet demand
baroda-etrade-str.description: Demand across segments (PV, CV & E-2W) remains structurally strong, led by small cars that jumped 3x post GST rate rationalization

baroda-etrade-str.title: Oil & Gas: Q4FY26 Preview – Sector performance to be mixed as supply issues emerge
baroda-etrade-str.description: Crude price volatility and LNG/LPG supply issues from the Middle East likely to weigh on performance. Negative impact on utility companies

baroda-etrade-str.title: Metals & Mining: Q4FY26 Preview – Price increase to benefit performance of Steel
baroda-etrade-str.description: Demand likely to remain good with 6.0-7.0%YoY growth, driven by strong demand from construction and auto sector

baroda-etrade-str.title: [Sector Report] Real Estate (Developers): Favour scale and execution in a normalising market cycle
baroda-etrade-str.description: Market entering a phase of hyper-supply; expect moderation in avg. realisations (in line with CPI) driven by the recalibration of demand
